channel.description: Recent content in Blackarch on Null Blog

item.title: Installing Arch Linux with LUKS2, Btrfs, and the BlackArch Repository
item.description:
explaoning the installation

parition

make file system

mount and make btrfs subvolumes

bootstrap arch-linux

chroot

set time and date

set locales

make user

install grub and edit init ramfs

optional network audio ect

item.title: Installing Arch Linux with LUKS2, Btrfs, and the BlackArch Repository
item.description:
Overview

This guide demonstrates how to install Arch Linux with the following configuration:

  • GPT partition table
  • UEFI boot
  • LUKS2 full disk encryption
  • Btrfs filesystem
  • Multiple Btrfs subvolumes
  • GRUB bootloader
  • PipeWire audio
  • NetworkManager
  • BlackArch repository

Final Layout

Partition Filesystem Purpose
EFI FAT32 EFI System Partition
Boot EXT4 Kernel and bootloader
LUKS2 Btrfs Root filesystem

Btrfs Subvolumes

Subvolume Mount Point
@ /
@home /home
@opt /opt
@cache /var/cache
@log /var/log
@libvirt /var/lib/libvirt
@containers /var/lib/containers

item.title: Installing Arch Linux with LUKS2, Btrfs, and the BlackArch Repository
item.description:
Installing Arch Linux with LUKS2, Btrfs, and the BlackArch Repository

This guide explains how to install Arch Linux using:

  • GPT partitioning
  • LUKS2 full-disk encryption
  • Btrfs with multiple subvolumes
  • GRUB as the bootloader
  • UEFI firmware
  • BlackArch repository (optional)

The layout is designed to work well with Timeshift snapshots while also separating logs, caches, virtual machines, and containers into their own Btrfs subvolumes.

explaoning the installation

This tutorial assumes:

  • You booted the official Arch Linux ISO.
  • Your system uses UEFI.
  • You want GPT partitioning.
  • You are comfortable using the terminal.

First, identify your storage device.
